patterns.name: Preposition Mix-Up
patterns.what: Sometimes mixes up which preposition naturally pairs with certain action words or time phrases.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Shayane chose 'of' instead of 'on' for the phrase 'congratulated on'.
Shayane used 'since' instead of 'about' when describing a conclusion.
Shayane picked 'by' instead of 'since' to show something happening from ancient times until now.

patterns.strategic_advice: When learning new verbs or nouns, try to memorize the preposition that goes with them as a pair (e.g., **'congratulate on'**, **'conclusion about'**). For time phrases, remember that **'since'** is used for a starting point in the past.

patterns.name: Choosing the Exact Word
patterns.what: Occasionally picks a vocabulary word that is related to the topic but doesn't perfectly match the exact context.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Shayane picked 'defeated' instead of 'stumped' to describe being confused by a clue.
Shayane chose 'reaps' instead of 'yields' for what a garden produces.
Shayane selected 'retreated' instead of 'yanked' for quickly pulling a hand back.

patterns.strategic_advice: When two words seem similar, look at the clues around the blank. For example, a garden **'yields'** (produces) crops, while a farmer **'reaps'** (harvests) them. Try plugging your choice back into the sentence to see if it sounds natural.

patterns.name: Hunting for Details
patterns.what: Tends to overlook key condition words in the question when scanning posters or flyers for specific information.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: Shayane missed the word 'not' in the question and picked an option that was actually in the text.
Shayane overlooked the condition for the 'biggest savings' when checking the poster details.
Shayane picked a general action instead of the exact step required to become a finalist.

patterns.strategic_advice:
When you see these prompts, treat them as stop signs:
- **'does not'** or **'except'** → highlight the negative word so you don't look for the opposite
- **'biggest'** or **'most'** → compare all the options in the text before settling on the first one you see
- **'steps required'** → trace the exact instructions in the text step-by-step
